title: Detecting Eulerian Circuit with Adjacency Matrix

statement.markdown: Given an adjacency matrix `g` representing an undirected graph, implement a method to check if the graph has an Eulerian circuit and return it. A graph is said to have an Eulerian circuit if it is connected and every vertex has even degree. The method should return the Eulerian circuit as a string of vertices in the order they appear in a circuit.
statement.inputFormat: The first input will be an integer `n` representing the number of vertices in the graph. The next `n` lines will represent the adjacency matrix `g` where `g[i][j]` is 1 if there is an edge between vertices `i` and `j`.
statement.outputFormat: If the graph has an Eulerian circuit, return a string of vertices in the order they appear in a circuit. Otherwise, return an empty string.
statement.notes: You are not allowed to use any external libraries or modules.

constraints.description: Number of vertices in the graph

examples.explanation: The graph has an Eulerian circuit: 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 1

hints.text: Use a depth-first search to traverse the graph and find an Eulerian circuit

editorial.title: Detecting Eulerian Circuit with Adjacency Matrix
editorial.intuition: An Eulerian circuit exists if and only if every vertex has even degree and the graph is connected. Use depth-first search to traverse the graph and find an Eulerian circuit.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force all possible paths in the graph to find an Eulerian circuit
editorial.betterSolution: Use depth-first search to traverse the graph and find an Eulerian circuit
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement a depth-first search algorithm to traverse the graph and find an Eulerian circuit
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The algorithm works by exploring all vertices and backtracking when a dead end is reached. If a circuit is found, return it.
editorial.dryRun: Start with a given graph and explore all vertices using depth-first search
commonMistakes: Not checking for even degree of all vertices before traversing the graph
Not handling the case where the graph is disconnected
